title: Arithmetic Operators Check
description: Operator precedence, `%`, and string concatenation with `+`.

questions.explanation: `*` before `+`; parentheses override; string `+` concatenates left to right; `%` is remainder.
Parentheses force numeric addition before concatenation with the string.
When one operand is a string, `+` concatenates. Parse with `Number()` before math.

questions.prompt: How do you test whether `n` is even?
questions.explanation: Modulo `%` gives the remainder. Even numbers have remainder 0 when divided by 2.

questions.prompt: What is the result of `10 / 4` in JavaScript?
questions.explanation: JavaScript `/` always produces a float when needed — there is no separate integer division.
